articles.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
articles.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

articles.title: Robinhood says its AI agent feature will ‘soon’ be assisting crypto traders
articles.summary: More than 70,000 agentic accounts have already been created by Robinhood equities and options traders since late May, when Robinhood launched a beta version of the product.

articles.title: Housing bill that includes a CBDC ban passed into law without Trump’s signature
articles.summary: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which includes a provision that bans the Fed from issuing a CBDC, has become law.

articles.title: Bitcoin analysts predict $300,000–$500,000 price in 2029. The math says no
DOJ moves to dismiss charges against alleged $722M BitClub fraudster: Report
articles.summary: Matthew Goettsche was set to face trial in October for conspiracy to commit wire fraud and selling unregistered securities.

articles.title: Apple Sues OpenAI, Claims Former Employees Stole Trade Secrets
articles.summary: Apple alleges former employees took confidential designs, supplier information, and engineering files before joining OpenAI.

articles.title: Meta's Chief Data Officer Says Agentic Commerce is the "Next Tier of Business"
Kraken to overhaul app with AI investing assistant
articles.summary: The redesigned app will use AI to recommend trades and tailor investing tools around users' financial goals as Kraken expands into broader financial services.

articles.title: Senate Democrats call for hearings into Trump’s ties to crypto amid CLARITY Act discussions
articles.summary: Five senators called for committee hearings to investigate US President Donald Trump’s policies potentially being influenced by crypto funding from United Arab Emirates-linked and other entities.

articles.title: Democrats Call for Senate Hearings on Trump's Massive Crypto Profits
articles.summary: Top Democrats on key Senate committees demanded inquiries into the more than $1.2 billion that President Trump made on crypto last year.

articles.title: U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns
articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in July, Address Ethics Concerns
Rep. French Hill urged the Senate to vote on the CLARITY Act before the August recess, arguing the bill would establish a transparent crypto market framework that addresses ethics concerns surrounding President Trump's digital asset ventures.

articles.title: DOJ to drop charges against man accused in $722 million BitClub crypto Ponzi scheme: Bloomberg
articles.summary: A former contestant on 'The Apprentice' is one of the persons urging the DOJ to drop the charges, the report said.

articles.title: Here’s what’s next for the Clarity Act as Congress returns to Washington
articles.summary: Congress returns to Washington next week, ready to take up cryptocurrency legislation before the midterm election calendar takes over.

articles.title: US CBDC ban to go into effect without Trump signoff on housing bill
articles.summary: Donald Trump confirmed on social media that he would not sign the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which includes a ban on a US CBDC until the end of 2030, setting the stage for the bill to become law on Saturday.

articles.title: Bitcoin Treasury Firm Empery Digital Dumps Nearly Half of BTC Holdings for $87 Million
articles.summary: Nasdaq-listed Empery Digital said it sold 1,400 Bitcoin since May to help fuel an AI data center deal, legal bills, and other expenses.

articles.title: SK Hynix’s $26.5 billion US listing brought to Telegram users via xStocks
articles.summary: Solana said that tokenized SK Hynix shares on SOL can be acquired through Backpack, xStocks and Ondo Finance.

articles.title: Xbox CEO Joins Fed AI Jobs Task Force Days After Announcing 3,200 Layoffs
articles.summary: Asha Sharma will advise the Federal Reserve on AI’s impact on jobs and productivity as Xbox undergoes the biggest restructuring in its history.

articles.title: New Hampshire council votes down $100M Bitcoin bonds
articles.summary: State representative Keith Ammon said the New Hampshire executive council's vote against Bitcoin-backed bonds was “short-sighted,” urging the body to reconsider the measure.

articles.title: Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank
Circle won final OCC approval to establish a national trust bank, allowing it to place USDC reserves under federal oversight and expanding its regulated digital asset custody business.
This post Circle (CRCL) Wins Final OCC Approval for National Trust Bank first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

articles.title: Empery Digital offloads 1,400 bitcoins to help fund AI pivot, pay debt
articles.summary: Empery Digital used part of the proceeds for a 25% stake in an AI data center campus project.

articles.title: Backpack joins race for 24/7 stock markets with tokenized equities
articles.summary: The launch comes as tokenized stocks become one of the fastest-growing corners of crypto, with exchanges and traditional finance firms racing to expand 24/7 trading.

articles.title: Kraken to relaunch its mobile app with agentic trading and advice at the center
articles.summary: Kraken is adding autonomous AI agents capable of monitoring markets and suggesting trades for users.

articles.title: Cambridge research puts 31% of Ethereum node activity in the US, where a third offline can stall finalization
articles.summary: Cambridge finds 31% of Ethereum node activity in the U.S., clustered on AWS, Hetzner and OVH, raising jurisdictional/counterparty questions.

articles.title: U.S. government digital dollar set to be banned tonight under housing law's CBDC limit

Bitcoin Magazine

Metaplanet is taking its Bitcoin strategy beyond treasury accumulation, launching a study to explore tokenized, Bitcoin-backed credit products that could reshape Japan's debt market.

articles.title: Trump Won't Sign Housing Bill With CBDC Ban—Will It Become Law Tonight Anyway?
articles.summary: Unless President Trump vetoes the bill, it will become law at midnight, banning the Federal Reserve from developing a CBDC until 2031.

articles.title: Bitcoin whales sent BTC price to $64K as Coinbase Premium broke key level: CryptoQuant
articles.summary: Bitcoin price gains came thanks to US whales propeling the Coinbase Premium above a key trend line, new analysis from CryptoQuant reported.

articles.title: Circle Stock Jumps as Stablecoin Issuer Wins Final Federal Banking Charter Approval
articles.summary: Circle secured final OCC approval to establish a national trust bank, shifting its $73.2 billion stablecoin to a unified federal framework.

articles.title: A16z’s Andreessen lands Federal Reserve role as AI reshapes policy debate
articles.summary: The Fed named a16z co-founder Marc Andreessen to co-lead an AI productivity and jobs task force under Chair Kevin Warsh’s policy review.

articles.title: MiCA licensing only the beginning as crypto custodians face scrutiny
articles.summary: A MiCA license allows crypto firms to operate in the EU, but the ESMA’s review will test whether custodians can meet the required security and resilience standards.

articles.title: Strategy Bitcoin Sales 'Mostly Noise,' Standard Chartered Says, Holding $100K BTC Call
articles.summary: The bank argues that the treasury giant's Bitcoin sales are a short-term distraction, reiterating its $100,000 year-end price target.

articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin is “A Screaming Buy”: Standard Chartered Backs $100,000 Target, Shrugs Off Strategy (MSTR) Sell-Off
Standard Chartered reaffirmed its $100,000 end-2026 Bitcoin price target, calling Bitcoin "a screaming buy" and arguing that Strategy's recent sell-off reflects a communication issue around its new monetization strategy rather than a weakening Bitcoin outlook.

articles.title: Will the crypto lobby's $189M campaign get CLARITY over the line?
articles.summary: As the CLARITY Act advances, crypto's growing political influence in Washington is under scrutiny. But the crypto lobby's massive campaign spend is only part of the story.

articles.title: Binance co-CEO says 70% of EU withdrawals went to self-custody after MiCA deadline, with just 30% going to licensed platforms
articles.summary: Richard Teng said 70% of EU user withdrawals following its service suspension moved to self-custody rather than MiCA-regulated platforms.

articles.title: ‘Communication challenge, nothing more’: Standard Chartered keeps $100,000 bitcoin target, calls Strategy selloff a signaling problem
articles.summary: Standard Chartered retained its $100,000 end-2026 bitcoin target, calling Strategy's STRC pivot a signaling problem, not a solvency one.

articles.title: Wall Street banks tighten prediction market rules for staff as insider fears spread
articles.summary: Wall Street banks, including Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, are restricting employee prediction market trades as insider trading fears spread across Polymarket and Kalshi.

articles.summary: Circle wins final OCC approval for First National Digital Currency Bank, with USDC reserve management planned for a later phase.

articles.title: Ledger researchers disclose Tangem card flaw; Tangem says risk to everyday users is ‘virtually non-existent’
articles.summary: Ledger researchers disclosed a laser attack that resets Tangem card passwords by bypassing a recovery-state check in the firmware.

articles.title: Revolut integrates its crypto exchange with AI assistants as agentic trading spreads
articles.summary: Revolut X now connects to Claude, Gemini, OpenClaw, and Cursor, letting crypto users analyze, backtest, and execute trades via AI prompts.

articles.title: North Carolina Bill Recognizes CFTC's ‘Federal Regulatory Authority’ Over Prediction Markets
articles.summary: The state's budget law leaves oversight of Kalshi and Polymarket to the CFTC and taxes them at 6%, far below what other states are pursuing.
